© 2025 Historic Free Fencing Federation. All rights reserved.

Who We Are

About Us & Mission

Elevating the HEMA tournament experience through structure, standards, and community.

Mission Statement

The Historic Free Fencing Federation is a collection of Schools and Clubs focused on the improvement of the Tournament Experience in HEMA (Historic European Martial Arts). Our approach to improvement is multi-layered, addressing rankings, skill development, officiating, event standards, and training resources in a unified and professional framework.


It is our belief that driving to meet these goals will make the HEMA Tournament and Event culture much more successful and professional, leading to a more positive experience for students and leaders in the community.

Our Five Pillars

1

Rankings System

An internal Rankings System that reflects defensive skills rather than reckless offense, promoting tiered improvements in skills for our fencers and rewarding smart, measured technique.

2

Skill Tiers

An overall Skill Tier system placing fencers from S Tier (the best) down to D Tier (untested), ensuring fencers have clear goals at tournament and encouraging ongoing participation and growth.

3

Officials Structure

A structured framework for officials and tournament organization — with standards for testing and honing the skills of referees, judges, table staff, and organizers, plus training pathways to meet those standards.

4

Event Standards

Standardized practices for HEMA events covering ring sizes, event formats, scoring modifiers, A-Tier event qualifiers, safety standards, and support for marketing and sponsorship.

5

Training Resources

A database of training resources for fencers — a video and document repository available to paid federation members, building a shared knowledge base for the community.

Historic Free Fencing Federation

Historic Free Fencing Federation Rankings

Historic Free Fencing Federation Rankings System

⚪ Loading...

Select Discipline

ℹ️ Rankings: View fencer rankings across all disciplines. Click fencer names for detailed profiles.
FMS (Fencer Mastery Score): FMS 0 = baseline skill level (1200 internally). Positive scores indicate above-average mastery.

⚔️ Welcome to the Arena!

🏆 What Are These Rankings?

The Historic Free Fencing Federation Rankings track your mastery across eight weapon disciplines! Every bout you fight, every exchange you win or lose, shapes your Fencer Mastery Score (FMS).

Think of it like your reputation in the guild — the more you fight and win, the higher your mastery climbs. Defeat skilled opponents? Your score soars! Face a tough loss? You'll learn and bounce back!

🎯 The Fencer Mastery Score (FMS)

Your FMS starts at 0 (representing a baseline skill of 1200). As you fight, your score changes based on:

  • Win against higher-ranked opponents? Big FMS gains! 🚀
  • Win against lower-ranked opponents? Small FMS gains
  • Lose to lower-ranked opponents? Larger FMS drops
  • Lose to higher-ranked opponents? Small FMS drops (they were expected to win!)
💡 Pro Tip: Your FMS updates every single round, not just at the end of the match! A 5-round bout means 5 chances to prove your skill.

🧮 The Math Behind The Magic

We use a battle-tested system called ELO rating (same math used in chess, esports, and competitive games worldwide!). Here's how it works:

Step 1: Predict the Outcome

Before each round, we calculate the expected probability of each fencer winning based on their current FMS difference.

Expected Win % = 1 / (1 + 10^((Opponent_FMS - Your_FMS) / 400))

If you have FMS +100 and opponent has FMS +50, you're expected to win ~57% of rounds

Step 2: Fight the Round!

The actual result: You win (1.0), you lose (0.0), or double loss (0.0 for both)

Note: A double loss means both fencers lose the round (both get 0.0). It's not a penalty - it's simply that neither fencer won that exchange.

Step 3: Update Your FMS

Your FMS changes based on whether you performed better or worse than expected!

FMS Change = 16 × (Actual Result - Expected Result)
Example:
  • You (FMS +50) vs Opponent (FMS +150)
  • Expected: You have ~36% chance to win
  • You WIN the round! (Upset victory!)
  • FMS Change: 16 × (1.0 - 0.36) = +10 FMS! 🎉

📊 What This Means For You

  • Beat stronger opponents: Massive FMS gains!
  • Defend your rank: Win expected matches to climb steadily
  • Learn from losses: Small FMS drops when losing to better fencers
  • Every round counts: Even in a losing match, winning rounds helps your score!

📈 Confidence Score

Not all FMS ratings are equally reliable! The Confidence Score shows how much data supports your rating.

How Confidence Works

Confidence increases with the number of matches you've fought:

  • 80-100% (Very High): 20+ matches - Your FMS is highly reliable!
  • 60-79% (High): 10-19 matches - Good data, solid rating
  • 40-59% (Medium): 5-9 matches - Getting there!
  • 20-39% (Low): 2-4 matches - Early days, rating may shift
  • 0-19% (Very Low): 0-1 matches - Not enough data yet
💡 Why It Matters: A fencer with FMS +50 and 80% confidence is more accurately rated than someone with FMS +100 and 20% confidence. The second fencer might be a beginner who got lucky in their first few matches!

📊 Adjusted FMS & Provisional Rankings

To prevent fencers with just a handful of matches from outranking proven veterans, we use Adjusted FMS for leaderboard placement.

The Adjusted FMS Formula

Adjusted FMS = Raw FMS × (Confidence ÷ 100)

Your raw FMS is never changed — only the ranking position uses the adjusted value.

How It Works In Practice

✅ Veteran (50 matches)
Raw FMS: +40
Confidence: 96%
Adjusted: +38 — barely changes!
⚠️ One-Event Wonder (3 matches)
Raw FMS: +45
Confidence: 18%
Adjusted: +8 — must prove it!

The veteran with +40 FMS over 50 matches rightfully outranks the newcomer with +45 FMS over 3 matches. As the newcomer fights more, their adjusted FMS climbs toward their raw score.

🏷️ Provisional Status

Fencers with fewer than 10 matches in a discipline are marked PROVISIONAL:

  • No numbered rank on the leaderboard (shown as "PROV")
  • Skill tier locked to D — Untested regardless of FMS
  • Raw FMS still visible on their profile and still updates each match
  • Once they hit 10 matches, they receive their full rank and tier
💡 Why? A 3-0 record at one event doesn't prove you're S-tier. Get out there, fight more bouts, and earn your rank the honest way!

📈 Confidence Growth by Match Count

3

FMS × 0.96

🔄 Hybrid Scoring System

This system uses a Hybrid Scoring approach that combines the best of both worlds:

FMS Change Formula

FMS Change = (Match Result × 70%) + (Round Changes × 30%)

70% Match-Based Component

  • Single FMS calculation for match outcome
  • Win = 1.0, Draw = 0.5, Loss = 0.0
  • Winning matters most
  • Primary driver of FMS change

30% Round-Based Component

  • FMS updates calculated for each round
  • Rewards defensive skill (survivability)
  • Differentiates quality of wins
  • 5-0 win ≠ 3-2 win

📊 Example: Hybrid Calculation

Player A (FMS +50) defeats Player B (FMS +100) with a score of 3-2:

Match Result: +18 FMS (upset victory)
Round Changes: +25 FMS (from 5 individual rounds)
───────────────────────────────
Hybrid = (18 × 0.7) + (25 × 0.3)
       = 12.6 + 7.5
       = +20.1 FMS
         (Match×70%)  (Rounds×30%)
Floor: FMS cannot drop below -50
💡 Why 70% Match / 30% Rounds? The match outcome is the primary driver of FMS — winning matters most. Round performance still contributes granularity, rewarding dominant 3-0 wins slightly more than close 3-2 victories. The -50 floor prevents discouraging deep negative spirals for developing fencers.

🎖️ Additional Stats We Track

📈
Match Record

Wins-Draws-Losses

🛡️
Survivability

% rounds you didn't get hit

(Wins + Double Losses)

⚔️
Round Stats

Wins-Losses-Double Losses

DL = both fencers lose that round

🏆
Peak FMS

Your highest rating ever

⚔️ Now Get Out There and Fight! ⚔️

Every bout is a chance to prove your skill.
Every round shapes your legacy.
Your mastery awaits!

ℹ️ Add Fencers: New fencers start at 1200 Fencer Mastery Score. You can assign them to a school/club and specific discipline.

Add New Fencer

📦 Backup & Restore

Export all data (fencers, matches, rankings) to a single file for backup or sharing across devices/clubs. Import to restore or merge data from another system.

💡 Tip: Export regularly to backup your data. Share the exported file with other clubs to merge rankings!

📊 Import from Excel/CSV

ℹ️ Import Format: Excel/CSV can include fencers only OR fencers with match data
Fencer Import: Name, School/Club, Discipline
Match Import: P1, P1 Rounds Won, P1 School/Club, P2, P2 School/Club, P2 Rounds Won, Double Losses, Pool Stage, Event (discipline), Tournament, Match Win (optional: Y=P1 won, N=P2 won)
⚠️ Scoring Formula: 70% match-based + 30% round-based (FMS floor: -50)
Event/Discipline: Accepts variations like "Men's Longsword Open" → Longsword, "Stout Sword" → Sword and Offhand
Valid disciplines: Longsword, Side Sword, Single Rapier, Sword and Buckler, Messer, Dagger, Saber, Rapier and Offhand, Sword and Offhand, Harness, Smallsword

Import Excel/CSV Data

Expected Columns:

Fencer Import (3 columns):

  1. Name
  2. School/Club
  3. Discipline

Preview

⚙ Recalculate All FMS

Reset all fencer Elo ratings to baseline and replay every recorded match through the current formula (70% match-based + 30% round-based, floor at -50). Use this after changing scoring parameters or to fix data inconsistencies.

ℹ️ Scoring: 70% match outcome + 30% round performance | FMS floor: -50 | Win = 1 | Loss = 0 | Double Loss = 0 (both lose)

HomeSkill Tiers

© 2025 Historic Free Fencing Federation. All rights reserved.

Fencer Classification

Skill Tier List

Fencers progress through tiers based on FMS score, Confidence Rating, and Survivability.

📊 View the Full Rankings

Skill Tiers are determined by your Fencer Mastery Score (FMS) — a composite rating calculated from your win rate, round-level scoring, survivability, and confidence rating across all sanctioned federation events. Compete in more events, win more bouts, and your FMS rises — pushing you into higher tiers.

Open Rankings Tool ↗

🔄 3-Year Rolling Cycle

Skill Tier placement is based on a 4-year rolling window. Tournament data older than 4 years automatically falls off, and your FMS and Tier are recalculated. This ensures rankings reflect current competitive performance — not past glory. Stay active, keep competing, and your Tier will reflect who you are on the mat today.

📈 How to Advance

Tiers are evaluated after each sanctioned event. To climb, you need sustained FMS performance above your current tier's threshold across multiple events — a single strong showing won't jump you up, but consistent excellence will. Tier demotions are also possible if performance drops below thresholds, keeping the system honest and competitive.

Tier Classifications

Click on a tier to see the fencers currently placed in that classification.

S
S Tier — Grand Champion
Elite fencers demonstrating exceptional mastery of both offensive and defensive technique. Consistently high FMS scores across multiple sanctioned events. The standard-bearers of the federation.
⚔ FMS ≥ +130
A
A Tier — Advanced
Highly skilled fencers with strong competitive records. Excellent FMS performance demonstrating clear command of distance, timing, and tactical decision-making under pressure.
⚔ FMS ≥ +65
B
B Tier — Intermediate
Experienced fencers with a solid competitive foundation. Consistent event participation with improving FMS metrics. Beginning to demonstrate reliable defensive patterns alongside effective offense.
⚔ FMS ≥ +15
C
C Tier — Developing
Fencers actively building their competitive toolkit. Tournament experience is accumulating and patterns are emerging in FMS data. Clear areas for improvement identified through ranking data.
⚔ FMS ≥ -20
D
D Tier — Untested
Fencers with no tournament experience or insufficient data to generate a reliable FMS score. D Tier is the entry point — every S Tier fencer started here. Compete, log results, and begin the climb.
⚔ ENTRY — NO MINIMUM

Structure & Standards

Officials & Standards

Developing the next generation of skilled, credentialed HEMA officials.

Official Roles

The HFFF provides a structured pathway for all event officials — ensuring that referees, judges, table staff, and organizers meet consistent standards that protect fencers and uphold the integrity of competition.

Referees

Testing standards, certifications, and ongoing development for bout referees. Training covers rules application, decision speed, and conflict resolution.

Judges

Standards for edge judges and corner judges, including calibration training and consistency testing across events.

Table Staff

Scoring, bracketing, time-keeping, and administrative roles. Standardized training ensures smooth event execution.

Organizers

Event planning, venue coordination, safety oversight, and federation compliance. Organizer certification pathways coming soon.

Officials Apparel

All HFFF-certified officials are identified at events by the official black polo — featuring the HFFF seal on the chest and OFFICIAL printed on the sleeve and back. This ensures fencers, coaches, and spectators can immediately identify credentialed federation personnel on the floor.

Standards Documentation

Full written standards, testing procedures, and certification requirements are being finalized and will be published here shortly.

HomeEvent Standards

© 2025 Historic Free Fencing Federation. All rights reserved.

Event Excellence

Events & Standards

Standardized practices that define what a professional HEMA event looks like.

HFFF Event Standards

The federation is developing comprehensive written standards covering all aspects of sanctioned HEMA events, ensuring consistency, safety, and professionalism across all federation-affiliated competitions.

Ring & Venue

Standardized ring sizes, surface specifications, safety buffer zones, and venue layout requirements for all HFFF-sanctioned events.

Event Formats

Pool structures, bracket types, round formats, scoring modifiers, and time-keeping standards for consistent competition delivery.

A-Tier Qualifications

The criteria that elevate an event to A-Tier status — ensuring only the highest-quality, best-organized competitions receive that designation.

Safety Standards

Minimum protective equipment requirements, medical presence, incident reporting procedures, and gear inspection protocols.

Marketing & Sponsorship

Federation support for event promotion, sponsor acquisition, and media presence to help events grow their reach and professional profile.

Full Standards Document

Comprehensive event standards are being finalized and will be published here for all member clubs and event organizers.

© 2025 Historic Free Fencing Federation. All rights reserved.

Welcome to the Art

What is HEMA?

Historical European Martial Arts — the revival of Europe's forgotten fighting traditions.

The Lost Martial Arts of Europe

Imagine picking up a longsword — not a prop, not a toy, but a real weapon of war — and learning to wield it the way a medieval knight would have. Not from a movie. Not from a video game. From the actual handwritten manuals left behind by the masters who taught kings, soldiers, and duelists how to fight and survive.

That's HEMA — Historical European Martial Arts. It's the modern study and practice of Europe's lost fighting systems, reconstructed from original manuscripts and treatises dating from the 14th through 19th centuries. This isn't choreographed stage combat or Renaissance Faire reenactment. This is a real, full-contact martial art with competitive tournaments, international rankings, and a global community of thousands of practitioners.

⚔ Real Martial Arts. Real History. Real Steel.

In Eastern martial arts — karate, judo, kung fu — there's an unbroken chain of masters passing techniques down through generations. In Europe, that chain was broken. The old fighting arts evolved into sport fencing, or simply vanished. But the masters left books. Detailed manuscripts full of techniques, illustrations, and philosophy of combat. HEMA practitioners study those manuscripts, reconstruct the techniques, and then test them — against each other, with steel in hand.

The Weapons You'll Learn

HEMA covers a staggering range of weapons and combat styles. Here are some of the most popular disciplines practiced in clubs worldwide:

⚔️
Longsword
The iconic two-handed sword — the most popular HEMA weapon. Powerful cuts, precise thrusts, grapples, and disarms.
🤺
Rapier
The elegant dueling weapon of the Renaissance. Fast, deadly, and often paired with a dagger or cloak.
🗡️
Side Sword
A versatile cut-and-thrust sword. The battlefield predecessor to the rapier, equally at home in civilian defense.
🛡️
Sword & Buckler
A nimble short sword paired with a small shield. One of the oldest recorded combat systems in Europe.
🔪
Messer & Dagger
The German long knife and the close-quarters dagger — personal defense weapons of the common citizen.
⚔️
Saber
The curved military sword. From Hungarian hussars to Napoleonic officers — fast, brutal, and decisive.

The Masters Who Left the Manuals

Everything in HEMA traces back to the original fight books — called Fechtbücher in German or treatises in English. These are the surviving written works of historical fencing masters, some dating back over 600 years. Here are a few of the giants whose teachings still guide HEMA practitioners today:

Johannes Liechtenauer

The grand master of the German longsword tradition. His teachings, encoded in cryptic verse, were passed down through dozens of manuscripts spanning nearly three centuries. Almost every German HEMA practitioner today studies his lineage.

Fiore dei Liberi

An Italian master who wrote Fior di Battaglia (The Flower of Battle) around 1409. His work covers unarmed combat, dagger, longsword, polearms, and armored fighting — all beautifully illustrated.

Joachim Meyer

A 16th-century German master who authored one of the most detailed and accessible Fechtbücher ever written. His 1570 treatise covers longsword, dussack, rapier, polearms, and dagger with beautiful woodcut illustrations.

George Silver

An English gentleman who championed the English school of swordsmanship against the Italian rapier craze. His 1599 Paradoxes of Defence remains one of the most passionate treatises on combat philosophy ever written.

📚 Explore the Original Manuscripts

Wiktenauer is the world's largest free library of HEMA manuscripts and translations. It hosts hundreds of original fight books with transcriptions, translations, and historical illustrations — all freely accessible. If you want to see what the old masters actually wrote, this is where you start.

Visit Wiktenauer ↗

What to Expect at Your First Class

Walking into a HEMA club for the first time can feel intimidating — people in fencing masks swinging steel swords at each other tends to have that effect. But here's the truth: HEMA is one of the most welcoming martial arts communities in the world. Most clubs are built by enthusiasts who are genuinely excited to share what they've learned.

Your first few sessions will typically involve footwork drills, basic cuts and guards, and partnered exercises with safe training swords (called feders or nylon wasters). No prior martial arts experience is required. Loaner gear is usually available. All you need is curiosity, a willingness to learn, and enough fitness to swing a sword for an hour.

Over time, you'll progress into sparring, tournament competition, and deeper study of the historical sources. Some practitioners focus on competitive fighting; others love the historical research. Many do both. That's the beauty of HEMA — it's equal parts martial art, history, and community.

Ready to Pick Up a Sword?

Find a HEMA school or club near you and take the first step into Europe's forgotten martial arts.

Find a HEMA School or Club Near You!
